bibliographic_data.title: A Novel comprehensive method for real time Video Motion Detection Surveillance
bibliographic_data.abstract: This article describes a comprehensive system for surveillance and monitoring applications. The development of an efficient real time video motion detection system is motivated by their potential for deployment in the areas where security is the main concern. The paper presents a platform for real time video motion detection and subsequent generation of an alarm condition as set by the parameters of the control system. The prototype consists of a mobile platform mounted with RF camera which provides continuous feedback of the environment. The received visual information is then analyzed by user for appropriate control action, thus enabling the user to operate the system from a remote location. The system is also equipped with the ability to process the image of an object and generate control signals which are automatically transmitted to the mobile platform to track the object.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper presents a low-cost, real-time video motion detection system that can be controlled from a remote location and used for tracking objects of predefined colors.
core_content.key_methodology: The system uses background subtraction, optical flow, and temporal differencing techniques to detect motion in the environment. It is designed with multiple modes of operation including PC-controlled mode, internet-controlled mode, tracing mode, and motion detection mode.
outcomes: Developed a low-cost real-time video motion detection surveillance system
Implemented various control modes for remote operation
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper introduces a comprehensive system for real-time video motion detection in surveillance applications. The system is designed to be efficient and can operate from a remote location, making it suitable for security concerns. It uses background subtraction, optical flow, and temporal differencing techniques to detect motion. The system operates in four independent modes: PC-controlled mode, internet-controlled mode, tracing mode, and motion detection mode. Each mode has specific functionalities such as controlling the mobile platform using a GUI or tracking objects of predefined colors.
